payload.source_text: The pocket money tracker system allows children to claim pocket money tasks as they are completed throughout the week.
Parents can detail tasks that should be completed and the monetary amount earned for that task.
As a task is completed it is linked to the child that completed it.
The children should be able to see the available tasks and the tasks they have completed that week.
At the end of each week the children can see the total amount they have earned that week.
The Parents can also see a report of how much each child has earned and the tasks they completed so that they can provide the pocket money earned to each child.
At the start of the next week all tasks become available again to be claimed.
Historical task data shall be available.
The resulting system shall be able to operate on iOS 16, Android 9 and Windows 11 or later.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Source elements present in the ledger.

payload.evaluated_scope: All input material in this project

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL ZachmanCell elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: Task claiming capability enabling children to accept and register assigned work items.
payload.justification: Strategic function supporting child participation in task acquisition process.

payload.description: Task definition and compensation assignment capability for parent stakeholders.
payload.justification: Core administrative function enabling parental control over task and payment structures.

payload.description: Earnings calculation and summary reporting aggregating completed task values weekly.
payload.justification: Financial processing function strategically important for compensation transparency.

payload.description: Performance reporting and analytics generation for parental compensation decision support.
payload.justification: Strategic analytical function enabling evidence-based compensation distribution.

payload.description: Task claiming and completion tracking
payload.justification: SG049 describes enabling children to claim and track tasks, representing a core strategic function the enterprise must perform.

payload.description: Task definition and monetary value assignment
payload.justification: SG050 articulates the strategic process of defining compensated task assignments, representing a distinct enterprise function.

payload.description: Task inventory visibility provisioning
payload.justification: SG051 captures the purpose of providing visibility, representing a strategic information delivery function.

payload.description: Performance reporting and compensation calculation
payload.justification: SG053 establishes the need for performance reporting to support compensation, representing a strategic analytical and financial function.

payload.description: Pocket money task representing compensated work unit assignable to children.
payload.justification: Central strategic entity referenced across multiple signals as core transactional object.

payload.description: Monetary value associated with completed task representing compensation amount.
payload.justification: Financial attribute strategically important for task valuation and earnings calculation.

payload.description: Task completion status indicator distinguishing available from finished work items.
payload.justification: State attribute critical for task lifecycle management and visibility requirements.

payload.description: Child performance record aggregating task completion metrics for compensation purposes.
payload.justification: Strategic entity representing analytical construct for parental decision support.

payload.description: Task history archive maintaining longitudinal activity records for accountability and analysis.
payload.justification: Historical data entity supporting governance and trend analysis strategic objectives.

payload.description: Compensated household task with assigned monetary value
payload.justification: Multiple signals reference tasks as things of strategic importance with associated monetary compensation, constituting a core enterprise entity.

payload.description: Child performance record for compensation determination
payload.justification: SG053 identifies performance reporting as a strategic thing enabling accurate compensation, representing a distinct tracked entity.

payload.description: Longitudinal task execution history dataset

payload.description: Weekly earnings accumulation total

payload.description: Weekly recurring period defining task tracking and earnings calculation intervals.
payload.justification: Temporal cycle establishing strategic rhythm for task management and compensation activities.

payload.description: Weekly task claiming and compensation settlement cycle
payload.justification: SG049 and SG052 both reference weekly temporal patterns for task tracking and earnings calculation, establishing a strategic time-based cycle.

payload.description: Children functioning as task claimants and earnings recipients within the pocket money system.
payload.justification: Multiple signals identify children as active participants with specific system interaction patterns.

payload.description: Parents serving as task definers, compensation assignors, and performance evaluators.
payload.justification: Signals establish parental role as administrative authority for task and compensation management.

payload.description: Child task performer and earnings claimant
payload.justification: Multiple signals identify children as active stakeholders who claim tasks, view inventory, and access earnings data, representing a distinct strategic role.

payload.description: Parental task assigner and compensation administrator
payload.justification: SG050 and SG053 establish parents as stakeholders responsible for task definition, assignment, and performance-based payment distribution.

payload.description: Enable children to view available task opportunities, claim assignments, monitor completion status, and access their completion history on a weekly cadence.
payload.justification: Signal expresses fundamental purpose aligned with strategic goal of child task tracking capability. | Strategic purpose focused on transparency of task inventory state for child stakeholders.

payload.justification: Articulates parental strategic objective for defining remunerated work structures.

payload.description: Enable children to access aggregated weekly earnings information for their completed work.
payload.justification: Motivational strategic goal centered on financial transparency and reward visibility.

payload.description: Establish longitudinal task activity records and performance analytics accessible to parents and stakeholders for accountability verification, behavioral trend identification, and justified compensation distribution.
payload.justification: Strategic need for performance reporting supporting parental compensation decision-making. | Strategic requirement for historical data access supporting governance and pattern analysis objectives.

payload.description: Enable children to claim completed household tasks and receive weekly monetary compensation
payload.justification: SG049 expresses a fundamental strategic purpose of the enterprise relating to task completion and compensation cycles, directly mapping to a strategic goal.

payload.description: Establish systematic definition and assignment of compensated household tasks with monetary values
payload.justification: SG050 articulates parental strategic motivation to create a structured task-compensation framework, representing a core enterprise goal.

payload.description: Provide comprehensive visibility of task inventory status to child stakeholders
payload.justification: SG051 captures strategic purpose of transparency regarding task availability and completion state, constituting a visibility goal.

payload.description: Enable child stakeholders to access summaries of their weekly earnings accumulation
payload.justification: SG052 identifies motivational strategic intent to provide earnings transparency, representing a financial visibility goal.

payload.description: Support accurate compensation distribution through access to child performance metrics
payload.justification: SG053 establishes parental strategic need for performance reporting to ensure proper payment accuracy, representing an operational fairness goal.

payload.description: Provide longitudinal task execution history for accountability and performance trend analysis

payload.description: Task claiming is the business process by which children select and accept completed work for monetary reward tracking.
payload.justification: The core business concept described involves children claiming completed tasks, which is a distinct business process.

payload.description: Task specification is the process by which parents define tasks and assign associated monetary values.
payload.justification: Signal describes a parent capability to create and value tasks, which is a business process.

payload.description: Child visibility function provides access to view both available and completed tasks within the current time period.
payload.justification: This describes a business capability or function that enables information access for child actors.

payload.description: Weekly earnings aggregation function calculates and displays cumulative monetary totals for children.
payload.justification: This describes a computational business function that produces aggregated financial information.

payload.description: Parent reporting function displays child-task-earnings relationships to facilitate payment fulfillment.
payload.justification: This describes a business capability that generates information for parental decision-making and payment actions.

payload.description: Task availability resets to initial state at weekly interval boundaries.
payload.justification: This defines a cyclical business rule that governs task state transitions based on time.

payload.description: Historical task data must remain accessible and retrievable across all time periods.
payload.justification: This establishes a business rule mandating data retention and accessibility requirements.

payload.description: Task represents a unit of work that can be completed by children and has an associated monetary value.
payload.justification: Multiple signals reference task entities with attributes (monetary value) and states (available, completed). This is a core business entity in the system.

payload.description: Child represents a person who claims and completes tasks to earn monetary rewards.
payload.justification: Child is a distinct business entity that participates in task claiming, completion, and earnings tracking.

payload.description: Parent represents a person who creates tasks, assigns monetary values, and fulfills payments.
payload.justification: Parent is referenced as a distinct actor performing specific business functions related to task definition and payment.

payload.description: Monetary value is a property of a task that quantifies the reward amount.
payload.justification: The signal explicitly defines monetary value as an attribute associated with task entities.

payload.description: Task completion status indicates whether a task has been finished and is eligible for claiming.
payload.justification: Signals distinguish between available and completed tasks, indicating a state attribute that changes through business processes.

payload.description: Child-task completion linkage associates a completed task with the specific child who performed it.
payload.justification: Signal SG292 explicitly specifies the relationship between completed tasks and children, and SG295 references this relationship for reporting.

payload.description: Weekly earnings total represents the aggregated monetary amount a child has earned within a week.
payload.justification: This represents a derived or calculated entity that summarizes earnings over a temporal period.

payload.description: Historical task data preserves past task information across multiple time periods.
payload.justification: This represents a persistent data entity that maintains temporal records for historical reference.

payload.description: Weekly cycle defines the recurring time period for task availability, earnings aggregation, and data reset.
payload.justification: Multiple signals reference weekly temporal boundaries as the fundamental business cycle for task and earnings management.

payload.description: Task completion event occurs when a child finishes work, triggering linkage and earnings tracking.
payload.justification: Task completion represents a significant business event that initiates claiming and relationship establishment.

payload.description: Weekly boundary trigger initiates the reset of task availability to beginning state.
payload.justification: The weekly temporal boundary acts as a trigger that causes the business rule to execute a task reset action.

payload.description: Child acts as a participant who views tasks, completes work, and tracks earnings.
payload.justification: Multiple signals identify children as active participants performing specific business activities.

payload.description: Parent acts as a participant who defines tasks, assigns values, reviews reports, and fulfills payments.
payload.justification: Signals identify parents as actors with specific capabilities and responsibilities in the business context.

payload.description: Enable children to earn monetary rewards by completing assigned tasks.
payload.justification: The core business concept establishes the fundamental business objective of the system.

payload.description: Support parents in managing payment obligations to children based on verified task completion.
payload.justification: The parent reporting capability serves the business goal of enabling accurate payment fulfillment.

payload.description: Task earnings and completion history must remain accessible for accountability and reference purposes.
payload.justification: The mandate for historical data retention reflects a business principle of transparency and accountability.

payload.description: Children may only view tasks within the current temporal scope defined by weekly cycles.
payload.justification: The visibility requirement establishes a temporal constraint on information access for child actors.

payload.description: Compute Weekly Earnings: A logical function that aggregates task completion values over a weekly period to produce child earnings totals.
payload.justification: Temporal aggregation logic describes a computational function with weekly scope and earnings output.

payload.description: Generate Parent Report: A logical function that produces aggregated views of child earnings and task completion metrics for parent consumption.
payload.justification: Reporting interface describes a transformation function producing aggregated output for specific user role.

payload.description: Reset Weekly Task Availability: A logical function that restores task availability state at weekly cycle boundaries.
payload.justification: Periodic state reset logic defines a function that modifies task availability based on temporal cycle.

payload.description: Task Completion Linkage Rule: Tasks must be associated with child entities when completion events occur.
payload.justification: Association rule defines a normative constraint on how completion events relate to child entities.

payload.description: Child Task Visibility Rule: Child users may only access task collections explicitly assigned to them.
payload.justification: Visibility requirements define access control rules governing data access patterns.

payload.description: Historical Data Persistence Rule: Task records must remain logically accessible after completion for historical reference.
payload.justification: Retention requirement establishes a normative rule for data lifecycle management.

payload.description: Task: A logical entity representing a unit of work assignable to children with associated completion status and earned value.
payload.justification: Multiple signals reference task entities as the core data object with completion events, earnings, and weekly cycle properties.

payload.description: Child User: A logical entity representing the user role with task assignment, earnings accumulation, and access rights to assigned work.
payload.justification: Child entity identified through visibility requirements, earnings attribution, and task collection access patterns.

payload.description: Parent User: A logical entity representing the supervisory role with access to aggregated child performance and financial data.
payload.justification: Parent entity explicitly identified as recipient of aggregated reporting data.

payload.description: Earned Amount: A logical entity representing accumulated financial value resulting from task completion within a temporal period.
payload.justification: Earnings identified as distinct data entity subject to aggregation, computation, and reporting processes.

payload.description: Task-Child Assignment: Logical relationship binding task entities to child user entities with associated completion and earning attributes.
payload.justification: Association rule and visibility requirements define the logical linkage between tasks and child entities.

payload.description: Parent-Child Supervision: Logical relationship enabling parent entities to access aggregated data from child entities.
payload.justification: Reporting interface defines supervisory relationship structure between parent and child entities.

payload.description: Task Completion Status: A logical attribute indicating the completion state of a task entity.
payload.justification: Completion events and reporting requirements indicate task status as a key attribute.

payload.description: Task Historical Record: A logical attribute capturing temporal persistence of task state and completion data.
payload.justification: Retention requirement defines historical task data as a persistent attribute.

payload.description: Task Completion Event: A state transition occurring when a child completes an assigned task, triggering earnings calculation and record updates.
payload.justification: Completion events identified as temporal trigger points for association logic and earnings computation.

payload.description: Weekly Aggregation Cycle: A recurring temporal period defining the boundary for earnings computation, presentation, and task availability reset.
payload.justification: Multiple signals reference weekly periodicity as the fundamental cycle for system state transitions.

payload.description: Weekly Cycle End Trigger: A temporal trigger initiating task availability restoration and earnings presentation functions.
payload.justification: Periodic reset logic and aggregation timing define explicit trigger points for state-changing functions.

payload.description: Data Accessibility Constraint: Historical task records must remain logically accessible to support audit and reference requirements.
payload.justification: Retention requirement establishes a design constraint on data lifecycle and accessibility.

payload.description: Role-Based Access Constraint: Data visibility must be restricted according to user role relationships and assignment patterns.
payload.justification: Visibility requirements establish constraints on data access patterns based on logical user roles.

payload.description: Temporal Aggregation Principle: Financial and performance data should be computed and presented in consistent weekly periods aligned with cycle boundaries.
payload.justification: Weekly computation and cycle behavior reflect an underlying design principle for temporal data organization.

payload.description: Specific operating system versions required as deployment targets for the platform.
payload.justification: Operating system versions represent physical computing nodes in the technical infrastructure where the system executes. This is a Row 4 (Builder) physical node specification.

payload.description: Technical requirement limiting platform deployment to designated operating system versions.
payload.justification: The compatibility specification imposes a technical constraint on deployment options, restricting which OS versions are permissible. This is a normative constraint at the Builder level.

payload.description: Task visibility function providing child actor access to available and completed task lists within current weekly cycle.
payload.justification: SG541 describes visibility requirements. Recast as a detailed function specification defining how task viewing operates for child actors at implementation level.

payload.description: Report access function enabling parent actor retrieval of distribution data for pocket money calculation.
payload.justification: SG543 describes parent report access. Specified as a detailed function for retrieving and presenting distribution-relevant data to parent actors.

payload.description: Weekly task reset process clearing claimed status and restoring task availability at cycle boundary.
payload.trigger_condition: At start of new weekly cycle
payload.justification: SG544 indicates task availability reset timing. Elaborated as a detailed process specification describing the sequence of operations that restore task claiming state.

payload.description: Weekly operational cycle governing task reset timing and earned amount aggregation availability.
payload.justification: SG542 and SG544 both reference weekly timing boundaries. Combined to define the detailed weekly cycle specification controlling task availability reset and financial summary access points.

payload.description: Task claiming event executed by child actor during active weekly period.
payload.justification: SG539 describes temporal task claiming activity. Recast as a specific event specification defining when and how task claiming operations occur within the system timing model.

payload.description: Earned amount total access event triggered at weekly cycle boundary for child actor.
payload.justification: SG542 establishes timing for accessing totals. Specified as a discrete event tied to the weekly boundary condition at implementer detail level.

payload.description: iOS mobile platform deployment node specification.
payload.justification: SG545 identifies iOS as a deployment platform. Extracted as a distinct node specification for detailed deployment architecture.

payload.description: Android mobile platform deployment node specification.
payload.justification: SG545 identifies Android as a deployment platform. Extracted as a distinct node specification for detailed deployment architecture.

payload.description: Windows desktop platform deployment node specification.
payload.justification: SG545 identifies Windows as a deployment platform. Extracted as a distinct node specification for detailed deployment architecture.

payload.description: Child user interface for claiming tasks and viewing earned amounts within weekly operational periods.
payload.justification: SG539 and SG541 both describe child actor operational capabilities. Combined at implementer level to specify the detailed interface requirements for child user interactions with task claiming and viewing functions.

payload.description: Parent user interface for task configuration, monetary assignment, and distribution report access.
payload.justification: SG540 and SG543 define parent actor responsibilities. Consolidated to specify detailed parent interface requirements covering task management and reporting functions at the implementation level.

payload.title: High Level Description
payload.description: Section: High Level Description

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Segment elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: Expresses the fundamental purpose of enabling children to claim and track completed pocket money tasks weekly.
Articulates parental motivation to define compensated task assignments with associated monetary values.
Captures the purpose of providing children visibility into available and completed task inventory.
Identifies the motivational goal of enabling children to view their weekly earnings summary.
Establishes parental need to access child performance reporting for accurate compensation distribution purposes.
Indicates stakeholder requirement for longitudinal task history access supporting accountability or trend analysis.
Section header indicating the commencement of conceptual system description.
Establishes the core business concept of children claiming completed tasks for monetary reward tracking.
Defines the parent capability to specify task entities with associated monetary value attributes.
Specifies the relationship between completed task entities and child entities through linkage.
Defines child actor visibility requirements for both available and completed task entities within temporal scope.
Establishes weekly aggregation capability for earned monetary totals visible to child actors.
Defines parent reporting capability displaying child-task-earnings relationships to support payment fulfillment.
Specifies the cyclical business rule for task availability reset at weekly temporal boundaries.
Mandates retention and accessibility of historical task data entities across temporal periods.
Logical association rule defining how task completion events are linked to child entities.
Data visibility requirements specifying logical access patterns for child users to task collections.
Temporal aggregation logic defining weekly computation and presentation of earned amounts to children.
Logical reporting interface providing parents aggregated view of child earnings and task completion data.
Periodic state reset logic defining weekly cycle behavior for task availability restoration.
Data retention requirement mandating logical persistence and accessibility of historical task records.
Platform compatibility constraint specifying concrete operating system versions for deployment.
Identifies children as operational actors who execute task claiming activities throughout the week on a temporal basis.
Identifies parents as operational actors responsible for task configuration and monetary value assignment activities.
Defines operational visibility requirements for child actors viewing available and completed tasks within weekly cycles.
Establishes weekly operational schedule boundary when child actors can access earned amount totals.
Defines parent operational role in accessing reports to execute pocket money distribution activities.
Specifies weekly operational cycle timing when task availability resets for claiming operations.
Defines deployment platform locations specifying where the system operates across iOS, Android, and Windows environments.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Signal elements present in the ledger.
This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Stakeholder elements present in the ledger.
This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL CellContentItem elements present in the ledger.
